title: Gendo and Kaworu The Adam Connection
display_title: Gendo and Kaworu The Adam Connection
wikitext:
[]: Gendo and Kaworu: The Adam Connection             Quote Post by Cosman246 » Tue Dec 20, 2011 2:11 am I'm not sure whether this topic will be more appropriate here, or in Evangelion Discussions: it all depends on how it turns out. I myself see a connection between Kaworu and Gendo, much like the fact that there is a connection between Rei and Yui (though the actual mechanism of this connection may be different--to insinuate that Kaworu was a clone with some of Gendo's DNA as Rei was of Yui would be unsupported)--in the fact that Kaworu has Adam's spirit while Gendo has fused with Adam, and also in the way that Kaworu and Rei often appear to each other. What are your thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Dec 20, 2011 3:08 am If you're talking about the series then this is crazy. If you're talking about rebuild it's in the wrong forum. Either way you're being wrong on the internet.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Tue Dec 20, 2011 3:30 am I believe that the Commander and Kaworu only share some similarities, which are two facts. We know that Seele obtained both Adam's soul and embryo, the former was placed inside Kaworu, while the latter was fused to Gendo's palm after Kaji supplied it to him in epidode eight. We see this happen in the manga, when Gendo consumes it, but I trust that his method of fusing himself to the embryo was diffrent in NGE, although I do not recall seeing it. I think it may have been during the directors cut version of episode twenty-four. Those two facts are the only things that connect Kaworu and Gendo together, the two 'halfs' you could say, of Adam, his soul and his embryo. They are both things that have been placed together to make one.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Tue Dec 20, 2011 3:40 am From what we see in the show, I doubt they ever interacted. Like ReiAyanami25 said the only thing they have in common is the Adam embryo.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Feb 03, 2012 2:01 pm I dunno, even in the original, there was that angle being played up, much more in the rejected scripts, or the scene in the DC where Kaworu muses "Shinji-kuns's father... is like me?" it seems like a purely symbolic/thematic connection here, perhaps by way of Kaworu = Adam = dark, nebulous father archetype thing = Gendo, with that being part of the Kaworu/Rei paralelism, but infering more that we're supposed to make more than some sort of vague, mental connection would be going a bit far... Young!Gendo used to have a few mannerisms similar to Kaworu's, one being the perpetual defiant smile. ...Rebuild, of course, is a completely different Game. I wonder how the K/S fangirls will react if there's such a revelation... probably, they will act like it never happened/ say that they're following the original canon... Still, we never learned whose DNA was used in the contact experiment; Kaworu has no strongly apparent physical resemblance to any cast member, least of all Gendo with those gruff, hard features of his.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Feb 03, 2012 2:08 pm Kendrix wrote: I dunno, even in the original, there was that angle being played up, much more in the rejected scripts, or the scene in the DC where Kaworu muses "Shinji-kuns's father... is like me?"

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Sat Feb 04, 2012 2:32 pm I wouldn't be surprised if Kaworu was the result of Gendo's human DNA coming into contact with Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Feb 04, 2012 2:49 pm ...Which would pose the question... of course, Rebuild diverges from the original series in many ways, but it is similar in many more, so, if Rebuild were to reveal something that was left open in the original series in a way that does not conflict with it, would it be reasonable to asume that that revealed fact was also the case in the original? After all, it's the same makers and they did have a few scenes that made things that were already present in the original series (Rei having waited for Shinji to wake up after the Ramiel incident, EVa 01's arms being damaged (tough not quite as demolished as in rebuild) in the Sahaquiel battle)... Other such things, aside from the possible Reveal of Kaworu's genetic donor, is the appearance of Toji's sister and the existance of unique EVA 05/06 models which, in the original, got scrapped for parts, and the reason why Shinji walks around with a cassete player in bloody 2015.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Sun Feb 05, 2012 5:23 am Maybe Kaworu does have some relation to Gendo in NME, judging from his 'father' statement at Tabgha Base. But I think that in NGE, Kaworu and Gendo never communicated with one and other or even met each other. The suggestion of a connection between them in NGE, in my personal opinion, seems absurd and far-fetched. The only things in common with the two of them seem to be the two 'halves' of Adam that they both possess.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Feb 05, 2012 6:53 am Note that Rei and Yui were never shown interacting either, tough they technically could have been in EoE... There is one scene of a Rei Avatar eying EVA 01 with an uncertain look on her face before she flies away, but that's all... Makes one wonder what sort of opinion they had of each other... but as for the topics, don't get me wrong, as i said, I think we're supposed to make a vague mental connection at most... ...Any of you seen those rejected scripts? Apparently, Episode 24 went through about as much rewriting as 2.X there was supposed to be a scene where Shinji and Kaworu go smimming (or well, the latter tries to teach the former...) and Kaworu would tell him a story about "a man who lost his wife" Also, I don't know if it was the same script, but one of these had Gendo and Kaworu meeting (Lol, imagine if they shook hand - the thin fabric of a glove preventing Third Impact XD) and having a brief conversation like the one he had with Rei; The script didn't specify much there, but apparently, Kaworu was to say "This place is your cathedral to a person of the past."

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sun Feb 05, 2012 7:04 pm driftking18594 wrote: I wouldn't be surprised if Kaworu was the result of Gendo's human DNA coming into contact with Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Feb 06, 2012 5:23 pm chaosakita wrote: He was an accountant back then, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Feb 07, 2012 7:38 am Kendrix wrote: On many German fansites (where everybody loves Kaworu a lot), the most popular theory is that Keel is the daddy, so let's include him in the Game as well XD

[]:             Quote Post by Xavierla » Fri Dec 07, 2012 9:28 pm Shinji ikari himself is a clone too you know. Well that's based of of the Seele's Dream theory. According to the theory which surprisingly has loads of evidence to support it, Shinji is the result of taking gendo and yui's DNA and the DNA of the fusion between Tetsuo Shima and Akira. He contains the much less known third seed of life.

[]:             Quote Post by GetOvaDaBridge » Fri Dec 07, 2012 10:03 pm Kendrix wrote: On many German fansites (where everybody loves Kaworu a lot), the most popular theory is that Keel is the daddy, so let's include him in the Game as well XD

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Fri Dec 07, 2012 10:08 pm In some ways Gendo and Kawrou are oppisates. Gendo pushes Shinji away, will never open up to him, and manipulates him. Kawrou tried to get closer to Shinji, straight up tells him he loves him, and even his asking Shinji to kill him was motivated by the desire for Shinji to live. In the original Kawrou was an angel who was made more human, and Gendo was a human who gained angel abilities via adam. It's kinda interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 07, 2012 10:38 pm GetOvaDaBridge wrote: At least it explains how he got that visor and mechanical implants.

[]:             Quote Post by GetOvaDaBridge » Fri Dec 07, 2012 11:08 pm ^Sorry, I forgot to add in how he got the implants and visor for the injuries he got from the contact experiment. I feel like an idiot.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 07, 2012 11:45 pm GetOvaDaBridge wrote: ^Sorry, I forgot to add in how he got the implants and visor for the injuries he got from the contact experiment.
